headline: Holland America Line and Jefferson's Bourbon Make History with the First Bourbon Aged on Cruise Ships
summary: To celebrate International Whiskey Day today, Holland America Line and Jefferson's Bourbon are announcing a partnership for a first-of-its-kind collaboration in which Jefferson's Ocean Bourbon is aged at sea across the cruise line's fleet. This marks the distillery's maiden voyage partnering with a cruise line for its Ocean Aged at Sea® Bourbon series. The program expands Holland America Line's award-winning Single Barrel Program and marks a new milestone in its America's 250th celebration, whic

headline: Carnival shares fall despite beating first quarter estimates
summary: Investing.com -- On Friday, Carnival Corporation (NYSE: CCL) reported first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations, though shares fell as investors weighed higher fuel costs impacting the company’s outlook.

content.title: Carnival shares fall despite beating first quarter estimates
content.summary: Investing.com -- On Friday, Carnival Corporation (NYSE: CCL) reported first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations, though shares fell as investors weighed higher fuel costs impacting the company’s outlook.

headline: Carnival Beats Earnings Estimates, Posts Record Bookings. Why the Beaten-Down Stock Is Falling.
summary: The cruise operator reported adjusted earnings per share of 20 cents, beating analysts’ expectations of 18 cents, according to FactSet data.  It even said bookings for 2026 are up double digits, reporting a record booked position at historically high prices.  The problem for investors is that Carnival’s earnings cover the three months ending Feb.28—the day U.S. strikes on Iran began.

content.title: Carnival Beats Earnings Estimates, Posts Record Bookings. Why the Beaten-Down Stock Is Falling.
content.summary: The cruise operator reported adjusted earnings per share of 20 cents, beating analysts’ expectations of 18 cents, according to FactSet data.  It even said bookings for 2026 are up double digits, reporting a record booked position at historically high prices.  The problem for investors is that Carnival’s earnings cover the three months ending Feb.28—the day U.S. strikes on Iran began.

headline: Carnival (NYSE:CCL) Reports Q1 CY2026
summary: Cruise ship company Carnival (NYSE:CCL) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 6.1% year on year to $6.17 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.20 per share was 8.9% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Carnival (NYSE:CCL) Reports Q1 CY2026
content.summary: Cruise ship company Carnival (NYSE:CCL) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 6.1% year on year to $6.17 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.20 per share was 8.9% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Carnival: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
summary: MIAMI (AP) — Carnival Corp. CCL) on Friday reported fiscal first-quarter earnings of $258 million. On a per-share basis, the Miami-based company said it had net income of 19 cents.

content.title: Carnival: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
content.summary: MIAMI (AP) — Carnival Corp. CCL) on Friday reported fiscal first-quarter earnings of $258 million. On a per-share basis, the Miami-based company said it had net income of 19 cents.

headline: Carnival Stock Falls Despite Record Bookings. Rough Seas Lie Ahead.
summary: While demand may be strong, fuel prices are a big concern for Carnival—and a big unknown for investors—as the company doesn’t hedge its fuel spending.  Rivals  Royal Caribbean  and  Norwegian Cruise Line  do.  Carnival reported adjusted earnings per share of 20 cents, beating analysts’ expectations of 18 cents, according to FactSet data.
